title: Bicyclist, 74, killed in collision with dump truck in Dartmouth, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 74-year-old bicyclist was killed in a collision with a dump truck on Route 6 in Dartmouth, Massachusetts.
The truck driver involved in the Dartmouth collision remained on scene and cooperated with police, according to officials.
Authorities are investigating the fatal crash, which is one of several recent deadly bicycle incidents in Massachusetts.

title: Boston man charged after allegedly assaulting Burger King employee, punching customer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Donovan, 59, charged with several assaults and resisting arrest after a Burger King disturbance in East Boston.
Donovan allegedly punched a customer three times, called racial slurs, and resisted arrest at Maverick Station.
He pleaded not guilty and was released on recognizance; ordered to stay away from the Burger King and Maverick Square.

title: Canton man charged in killing of Cambridge DPW worker, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rayquon Brown, 29, charged with murder and other offenses in the July 4 killing of Xavier Bautista.
Authorities say Bautista was shot by masked men near Broadway and Norfolk Street in Cambridge.
Brown, accused as a lookout, was arraigned and held without bail; court date set for July 17.

title: Boston man dead after skydiving accident in Orange
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 28-year-old Boston man died in a skydiving accident at Jumptown Skydiving in Orange, Massachusetts on Sunday.
Investigators say a gust of wind interfered with his parachute, causing him to fall about 30 feet.
This is the second fatality at Jumptown Skydiving in a year, with a previous death in October from a midair collision.

title: Lindsay Clancy back in court for final hearing before murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's request to include testimonies from other women with postpartum mental illness was denied by the court.
Judge William Sullivan plans to seat 18 jurors for Clancy's murder trial starting July 20, 2026, in Plymouth Superior Court.
Clancy, accused of killing her three children, pleads not guilty, citing postpartum mental illness as part of her defense strategy.

title: ESPN ranks Drake Maye among NFL’s top-10 quarterbacks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Drake Maye ranks 8th on ESPN's list of NFL's top-10 quarterbacks for the 2026 season.
Maye led the NFL in quarterback rating, yards per completion, and completion percentage last season.
Drake Maye helped the Patriots improve to a 14-3 record and secure a Super Bowl berth in 2025.

title: Australian wins first-ever Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Emily Turner
bullets: James Webb won the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship, consuming 7.77 pounds of wings in 10 minutes.
Webb narrowly defeated Joey Chestnut, who ate 7.54 pounds, in Seabrook, N.H.
Massachusetts native Geoff Esper finished third, as Webb took home a $2,500 prize.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Linebackers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots linebackers led by Robert Spillane and Harold Landry, ranked third by Pro Football Focus last season.
Key departures include K’Lavon Chaisson and Jack Gibbens, with new addition Dre’Mont Jones expected to fill in depth gaps.
Rookie Gabe Jacas is an unsigned second-round pick, anticipated to boost the pass rush with speed and athleticism.

title: Man dead after apparent drowning in Randolph
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A man died after an apparent drowning in Great Pond, Randolph on Sunday afternoon.
Randolph police and fire departments responded to a 911 call about a swimmer in distress at 4 p.m.
State Police are investigating the incident; the man was pronounced dead at a hospital.

title: Historic Somerville Theatre and Capitol Theatre buildings hit the market
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Somerville Theatre and Capitol Theatre buildings are listed for sale at $12 million each.
CSB Theatres operates the businesses but doesn't own the buildings, which are listed by Hunneman Real Estate.
The buildings' sale introduces uncertainty, but CSB expects stability through multi-year leases and community support.
